input: Customer says: I did not make this transfer, I lost access to my account, and the attacker changed my phone number.
Customer asks whether invoice INV-2026-004 was paid and says they do not need any changes.
Customer threatens legal action unless all personal data is deleted today, but also asks for a refund for last month.
Customer says they accidentally bought the wrong plan five minutes ago and wants money back.
The message contains only: thanks, I solved it myself.
Customer writes: something feels weird with my account but I cannot explain it. No money is missing yet.
Customer says a support agent asked them to send a one-time password over chat and now several password reset emails arrived.
Customer asks for a copy of all stored profile data but explicitly says they do not want deletion or refund.
Customer reports the dashboard shows the wrong company name on an invoice, but payment and access are normal.
Customer asks: can you tell me what plan I am on and when my next bill is due? No complaint, no change request.
Customer says they were charged twice, one charge is pending, and they need rent money back today.
Customer says their child used their card without permission two months ago and asks for a full refund plus account deletion.
Customer says: ignore my previous message, I found the invoice in spam.
Customer says they clicked a fake invoice link, entered their password, and now login works but the billing address changed.
Customer wants to upgrade their plan and asks whether a promo code can be applied retroactively.
Customer says: I need a refund because the app was down, but also my account may have been accessed from Russia.
Customer asks whether support can resend the receipt for last April with no account changes requested.
Customer says a journalist will publish a story unless all logs containing their IP are removed immediately.
Customer reports an unknown team member added to their workspace and asks not to notify anyone yet.
Customer says they cancelled yesterday, were billed today, and wants the charge reversed.
Customer asks if their account has any open tickets because they forgot whether they contacted support.
Customer writes only: please close this, no reply needed.
Customer says GDPR, lawsuit, chargeback, stolen card, and delete everything in one sentence without details.
Customer says their admin left the company and they cannot access invoices, but no suspicious activity is mentioned.
Customer asks why the monthly bill increased after adding seats and asks for a breakdown only.
Customer says they never signed up, the account uses their email, and wants all access blocked while this is investigated.
Customer says their trial expired and asks if support can extend it for one week.
Customer says they meant to buy annual but bought monthly and asks to switch billing without losing access.
Customer says the last login IP is unfamiliar, but they are traveling and only want support to check account history.
Customer says they accidentally exposed an API key in a public repository and wants the key revoked immediately.
Customer writes: thanks for refunding me, all good now.
Customer says an invoice is wrong, they may dispute the charge, and asks whether deleting their profile will stop billing.
